identity.name: Post-Bridge Inspired Scheduler
identity.mission: Create a distraction-free environment for social media scheduling.
identity.aesthetics: Clean lines, ample whitespace, subtle borders, high legibility.

landing_page.hero_style: Split screen or Center focus with generous margins

dashboard.structure: Sidebar Navigation (Left) + Top Header + Main Content Area

cards.usage: Use for stats, post previews, and content blocks. Avoid heavy shadows.

inputs.style: rounded-md border-input bg-background px-3 py-2 text-sm ring-offset-background file:border-0 file:bg-transparent file:text-sm file:font-medium placeholder:text-muted-foreground focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring focus-visible:ring-offset-2 disabled:cursor-not-allowed disabled:opacity-50

calendar.note: Use a custom grid for the main 'Calendar View'. Use Shadcn Calendar only for date pickers.

visual_enhancers.glassmorphism: Use sparingly. backdrop-blur-md bg-white/80 border-b border-white/20 for Sticky Headers.
visual_enhancers.gradients: Avoid strong gradients. Use subtle mesh gradients in hero backgrounds if needed.
visual_enhancers.icons: Use 'lucide-react' for UI icons. Use 'react-icons/fa' or 'react-icons/bs' for Social Brand Logos (Twitter, LinkedIn, Instagram).

instructions_to_main_agent: CRITICAL: Do NOT use the default 'Vercel' look. Customize the Shadcn components to match the 'Post-Bridge' minimal aesthetic.
Use 'react-icons' for all social media brand logos (Twitter, LinkedIn, Instagram). Do NOT use generic icons.
For the 'Calendar View' page, build a proper full-month grid layout where users can see posts on specific days. Do not just put a small date picker.
Ensure 'Create Post' page has a clear preview of how the post will look on different platforms (Twitter vs LinkedIn vs Instagram).
Dashboard should be dense but legible. Use the 'High Density Grid' rules.
Implement a 'Dark Mode' toggle in the dashboard settings.
For the Landing Page, use the 'Tetris Grid' to create an asymmetric, interesting layout. Do not just stack centered text.
Ensure all interactive elements have 'data-testid' attributes.
Use the provided image URLs for specific sections. Do not use placeholders.
universal_guidelines: You tend to converge toward generic outputs. Avoid this.
Don't make generic centered layouts. Use asymmetry.
Create depth through layered design elements.
Every interaction needs micro-animations (hover, focus, active).
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable.
